Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport

Interface Transport

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractStreamTransport, AbstractTransport, TcpTransport, TlsTransport, WsTransport
public interface Transport extends AutoCloseable
Minimal transport SPI for the publisher.

A Transport is one full-duplex framed link to a Zenoh router: send a batch of bytes with send(byte[]), receive the next batch of bytes with receive(long, TimeUnit). The framing (length-prefix on stream transports, message-per-datagram on datagram transports) is entirely the transport's business; the caller sees whole batches in and out.

Threading contract:

  • send(byte[]) may be called from any thread and is expected to serialise internally (per-transport lock).
  • receive(long, TimeUnit) should be called from a single dedicated reader thread inside the session, but is safe to call from any thread as long as calls do not overlap.
  • close() may be called from any thread and unblocks in-flight receive(long, TimeUnit) callers with either null (clean shutdown) or a TransportException (dirty).

Blocking-I/O implementation only; no async / NIO abstraction on purpose (see design constraints in the module README).

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Close the link.
    void
    connect()
    Open the link.
    String
    describe()
    Human-readable description of the peer endpoint, e.g.
    boolean
    isOpen()
    true between successful connect() and any close (clean or dirty).
    byte[]
    receive(long timeout, TimeUnit unit)
    Take the next received batch from the reader inbox.
    void
    send(byte[] batch)
    Enqueue one batch of bytes to be written to the peer.

  • Method Details

    • connect

      void connect() throws TransportException
      Open the link. After this returns, send(byte[]) and receive(long, TimeUnit) become usable. May only be called once per instance.
      Throws:
      TransportException - on connect failure

    • send

      void send(byte[] batch) throws TransportException
      Enqueue one batch of bytes to be written to the peer. Blocks until the batch has been fully handed to the OS write buffer.
      Throws:
      TransportException - if the link is closed, or the write fails, or the batch exceeds the transport's frame cap

    • receive

      byte[] receive(long timeout, TimeUnit unit) throws TransportException, InterruptedException
      Take the next received batch from the reader inbox. Blocks up to the given timeout.
      Returns:
      the next batch, or null if the timeout elapsed with no batch available
      Throws:
      TransportException - if the reader ended with an error (peer close is signalled by returning null and isOpen() transitioning to false)
      InterruptedException - if the caller thread was interrupted

    • isOpen

      boolean isOpen()
      true between successful connect() and any close (clean or dirty).

    • close

      void close()
      Close the link. Idempotent: multiple calls are legal and only the first has effect. Unblocks any in-flight receive(long, TimeUnit) caller (with null for clean shutdown, or by propagating the underlying error for dirty).
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable

    • describe

      String describe()
      Human-readable description of the peer endpoint, e.g. "tcp/10.0.0.1:7447".
